nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal reefs, lagoons, and intertidal zones in the photic zone; typical of tropical Indo-Pacific shallow subtidal habitat

decorPreferences: loose placement on rockwork or rubble

avoidWith: heavily grazing herbivores that may consume the specimen before it establishes

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Purple Gracilaria (Gracilaria corticata). Shows deep maroon/purple branching thallus.
Secondary product image from Mosaic Macros listing.

summary: Purple Gracilaria (Gracilaria corticata) is a deep maroon-to-purple branching red macroalgae from the Indo-Pacific, belonging to the family Gracilariaceae. It is generally considered beginner-friendly and thrives in refugium setups under medium lighting and moderate flow.
careNotes:
Purple Gracilaria (Gracilaria corticata) is a red macroalgae (Rhodophyta) in the family Gracilariaceae, order Gracilariales — the same family as the most widely kept Gracilaria species in the hobby. It is also traded under the name Maroon Purple Gracilaria.

Dedicated published husbandry data for this specific species is sparse, so water parameters are drawn from family-level Gracilaria care and are consistent with established data from closely related species: temperature is generally advised in the range of 22–26°C (72–78°F), salinity 1.023–1.025 SG, pH 8.1–8.4, and alkalinity 8–12 dKH — standard reef parameters shared across the genus. These are the same values used for Red Ogo (Gracilaria parvispora, sw-algae-007), Pom Pom Gracilaria (Gracilaria hayi, sw-algae-006), Green Ogo (Gracilaria tikvahiae, sw-algae-013), Red Spaghetti Gracilaria (Gracilaria pacifica, sw-algae-015), Monkey Finger Gracilaria (Gracilaria salicornia, sw-algae-014), Thick Ogo (Gracilaria sp., sw-algae-003), and Flat-Branched Striped Gracilaria (Gracilaria mammaralis, sw-algae-042).

Lighting is expected to be medium intensity, consistent with other Gracilaria kept in refugium setups. Growth rate is anticipated to be fast, in keeping with the genus. The deep coloration may vary with lighting intensity — reduced light conditions may intensify the purple pigmentation, while higher PAR (light intensity) can shift color toward red-brown, as is common in red macroalgae.

Propagation is by simple fragmentation, the standard method for aquarium Gracilaria. Cuttings that detach or are trimmed will generally form new growth points when placed in adequate flow and lighting. This species is available in 1.5–3" cutting-on-plug and 5"+ cutting formats.

As with Gracilaria generally, herbivorous fish such as tangs and rabbitfish tend to graze it and may consume a new specimen before it establishes. No copper-based medications should be used in systems containing this or any macroalgae.

DO NOT BURY IN SAND. As a red macroalgae, Purple Gracilaria will MELT at the base if placed directly in a sand bed. Attach it to rockwork using cyanoacrylate (superglue), zip-tie, fishing line, or by wedging it into a crevice instead. Sand-bed placement is generally appropriate only for green macroalgae genera such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, as well as some calcified Dasycladales.
breedingNotes: In captivity, propagation is achieved through simple fragmentation: portions of the thallus that detach or are trimmed will generally establish new growth when placed in adequate flow and lighting. Sexual reproduction via alternation of gametophyte and sporophyte generations occurs in the wild but is not a documented propagation path in the hobby.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source. Confirms scientific name Gracilaria corticata, deep maroon/purple coloration, and branching growth form with more pronounced ridges than related Gracilaria. No water parameter or numeric husbandry data provided on product page.

additional.name: Sibling species cross-reference (Red Ogo, Pom Pom, Gracilariaceae genus-level)
additional.notes: Water parameters (temperatureC, pH, kH, salinity) extrapolated from established sibling-species data for Gracilaria parvispora (sw-algae-007) and Gracilaria hayi (sw-algae-006), both Gracilariaceae. Parameters are standard reef values confirmed across multiple trade sources for the genus.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
